description_long: This object is a vintage-style electric fan with a circular metal blade guard featuring a grid-like mesh pattern. The fan has a cylindrical motor housing with ventilation slots and a small knob on top, likely for speed adjustment. Its base is wide and round, providing stability, and the entire structure is finished in a reddish, weathered paint that gives it an aged, industrial look. The fan blades are metallic and slightly curved, visible through the protective grille. The rear of the fan shows a recessed area with some text or branding, and the overall build suggests durability and robustness. The design is utilitarian, suitable for placement in garages, workshops, or urban interiors.
description: A vintage, industrial electric fan with a circular metal grille, metallic blades, and a sturdy round base, finished in weathered red paint. The fan features a cylindrical motor housing and a visible adjustment knob.
description_view_0: Front view showing the circular metal grille, central hub, and all fan blades clearly.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the cylindrical motor housing, ventilation slots, and the depth of the grille.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the motor housing, recessed branding, and the support structure.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, showing the grille depth and base profile.
attribute_blade_guard_design: The blade guard is a circular metal mesh with a grid pattern, composed of intersecting radial and concentric bars, providing robust protection and visibility of the blades.
